description: This figure presents X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of four Fe–20Mn–9Al–1.5C austenitic low-density steels with different alloying additions: 3Cr (black), 2Ni (red), 2Ni3Cr (blue), and 2Ni5Cr (green), all subjected to identical thermomechanical processing and ultrafast cooling. The horizontal axis shows the diffraction angle 2θ from 30° to 100°, and the vertical axis shows intensity in arbitrary units. All four curves display identical sets of diffraction peaks indexed as the face-centered cubic (FCC) γ-austenite phase, specifically the (111), (200), (220), (311), and (222) reflections, with the γ(111) peak exhibiting the highest intensity. No additional peaks corresponding to body-centered cubic (BCC) ordered B2 phase or other secondary phases are detected in any of the alloys. The citation text confirms that the XRD analysis reveals a single equiaxed FCC γ phase in all samples. This figure supports the core conclusion that varying Ni and Cr contents within this compositional range does not alter the single-phase austenitic structure, establishing the baseline microstructural equivalence for subsequent mechanical property comparisons.
